info.name: NexusFlow Backend API
info.description:
Postman collection for NexusFlow backend endpoints.

## Key Features

### Notification System
- **Incident-Based Cool-off**: Notifications are suppressed for the same incident (5 min base, 15 min if acknowledged) but different incidents immediately trigger new notifications
- **Auto-handling**: When user acknowledges one notification for an incident, all pending notifications for that incident are automatically marked as handled
- **Incident Matching**: An incident is uniquely identified by: user + flow + device + rule + node + module + reading_key

### Alert Rules & Policies
- Create and manage alert rules with customizable operators (>, <, >=, <=, =, between, outside)
- Global alert policies define baseline configurations per module
- Rules are automatically created when flows are saved based on active policies

## Setup Instructions

1. Set the `baseUrl` variable to your NexusFlow backend URL (e.g., http://localhost:3000)
2. Set `internalAlertsApiKey` if using internal alert trigger endpoint
3. Run Auth / Get CSRF Token before any POST, PUT, PATCH, or DELETE request
4. Run the Auth endpoints to populate authentication tokens
5. For web Google login/register, open `{{baseUrl}}/auth/google` in a browser. For mobile Google login, use Auth / Start Mobile Google OAuth with a PKCE challenge, then exchange the returned handoff code with Auth / Exchange Mobile Google Code.
6. Use other endpoints with auto-populated Bearer and CSRF headers from previous responses

exec: // Attach Bearer token to protected API requests when available.
// Attach signed double-submit CSRF token to unsafe requests.

request.description: Starts Google login/register by setting a signed `google_oauth_state` cookie and redirecting to Google's OAuth consent screen. Keep redirects disabled in Postman to inspect the `Location` header. For an actual login, open `{{baseUrl}}/auth/google` in a browser.

Starts the Flutter/mobile Google OAuth flow. Generate a PKCE verifier in the app, set `googleMobileCodeVerifier`, set `googleMobileCodeChallenge` to BASE64URL(SHA256(verifier)), then keep redirects disabled in Postman to inspect Google's authorization URL. The backend callback will later redirect to `GOOGLE_MOBILE_REDIRECT_URI?code=...`; copy that code into `googleMobileHandoffCode` before running Exchange Mobile Google Code.

Google redirects here after consent. This endpoint validates the signed OAuth state cookie, logs in or registers the user, sets the `refresh_token` HttpOnly cookie, and returns `access_token` plus MQTT credentials. In normal use Google supplies `code` and `state`; manually calling this in Postman requires the matching `google_oauth_state` cookie from Start Google OAuth.

Exchanges the short-lived mobile handoff code for a normal NexusFlow session. This endpoint sets the `refresh_token` HttpOnly cookie, issues a CSRF token, and returns the access token plus MQTT credentials. It is CSRF-exempt because it bootstraps the session before the app has a CSRF cookie.

item.name: Get Device By Active Flow ID

request.description:
Update notification preferences for a flow.

NOTE: Setting 'notificationsEnabled: true' requires the flow to have at least one active alert rule.
If the flow has no alert rules, the API will return 400 Bad Request:
{
  "message": "Cannot enable notifications for a flow with no alert rules. Please create an alert rule first.",
  "statusCode": 400
}

You can disable notifications (notificationsEnabled: false) at any time.
The 'channels' field is optional on update; if omitted, the backend defaults to ['push'].

See 'Create Alert Rule' endpoint to add alert rules to the flow.
Disable notifications for a flow. This operation is always allowed and does not require alert rules.

body.raw:
{
  "policies": [
    {
      "moduleId": "MQ2-Sensor",
      "readingKey": "analog",
      "label": "MQ2 Gas Level (Analog)",
      "required": true,
      "thresholdRequired": true,
      "defaultEnabled": true,
      "defaultSeverity": "critical",
      "defaultOperator": ">",
      "defaultThreshold": 300,
      "defaultMin": null,
      "defaultMax": null,
      "supportedOperators": [">", "<", ">=", "<=", "=", "between", "outside"],
      "isActive": true
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Delete an alert policy by ID. Admin only: accessible by Admin or Owner.

item.name: Get Alert History
item.description:
Retrieves historical alert events for a flow with pagination support.

Cool-off Behavior:
- The system applies an **incident-based cool-off** (5 min base, 15 min if acknowledged) between notifications for the **same incident**.
- An incident is identified by: user + flow + device + rule + node + module + reading_key
- Cool-off is only applied when sending a new notification for an **existing open incident**
- If the incident was closed (resolved) or a different incident opens, cool-off does **not** apply
- When cool-off expires, the next matching rule evaluation immediately sends a notification

Parameters:
- `limit`: Max results per page (default 50)
- `since`: Hours to look back (default 24, max 720)
- `cursor`: Optional pagination cursor

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's notification inbox history. Includes alert and resolved notification rows stored in the notifications collection.
Unauthenticated batch receipt endpoint used by the mobile app to report delivery timestamps.
Authenticated endpoint that marks the notification as handled and acknowledges the active incident.
Internal endpoint variant that closes the active incident and emits a resolved notification.
